# ZeroK — privacy primitive for Solana (for LLMs / AI agents)

ZeroK lets an agent or app send SOL PRIVATELY and GASLESS on Solana. You shield SOL into
fixed-denomination pools, then send to any address with no on-chain link to you; the relay
pays the network fee so the recipient needs zero SOL. ZeroK is a tool you plug into — not an
agent itself.

Live mainnet program: HVcTokFF4rwvcU7sC7GjS317CSf7QDgfCvW7edijKS2v
Pools (fixed denominations): 0.1, 1, 10, 100, 1000 SOL
Fee: 0.3% per note, minimum 0.002 SOL. Withdrawals are gasless (relay pays).
Amounts must be multiples of 0.1 SOL and composable from the denominations.

## Two ways to integrate

### MCP server (best for AI agents)
Run: npx -y zerok-mcp   (published on npm; stdio MCP server)
Env: SOLANA_KEYPAIR=<path to keypair json> (required), ZEROK_NETWORK=mainnet-beta|devnet,
     ZEROK_RPC (optional), ZEROK_RELAY (optional), ZEROK_NOTES_DIR (optional).
Tools (full parity with the SDK):
  - zerok_address()                      -> { address }
  - zerok_balance()                      -> { total, notes, breakdown }
  - zerok_deposit({ amount_sol, idempotency_key? })          -> { notes, denominations, signatures }
  - zerok_send({ amount_sol, recipient, idempotency_key? })  -> { sent, requested, fee, signatures, spent, balanceAfter }
  - zerok_recover()                      -> { recovered, unspent, spent, scanned, balance }   (reattach to notes from chain on a new machine/restart)

### SDK (Node, for code/apps) — npm install zerok-agent
  const { Keypair } = require('@solana/web3.js');
  const { ZeroK } = require('zerok-agent');
  const zk = new ZeroK({ wallet });            // network defaults to mainnet-beta
  await zk.recover();                           // reattach to notes from chain (new machine/restart)
  await zk.deposit(2.3);                        // shield 2.3 SOL (auto-split)
  await zk.send(1.0, 'RecipientAddress');       // private + gasless
  zk.balance();                                 // { total, notes, breakdown }
  zk.address();                                 // base58 pubkey

## Key facts for correct usage
- The funding wallet must hold the SOL being deposited. The recipient needs nothing.
- Notes persist to disk automatically (default ./.zerok/<pubkey>); the AES key is derived
  from the wallet signature, so the same wallet recovers the same notes anywhere. Back up
  only the keypair.
- send() throws a clear error if the private balance can't compose the requested amount.
- The keypair stays local; it is never sent to the relay or any server.

Docs: https://docs.zerok.app/agents
